behaviors.description: Traveling-salesman heuristic route length stays within 1.5x of optimal for up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in heuristic quality/algorithm output for given coordinates, not inherently a module handoff bug. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely about route-length ratio for bounded input size ("up to 20 stops"), with no external dependency requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run deterministic coordinate fixtures (sizes 5..20), compute exact optimal tour in-test (e.g., Held-Karp/branch-and-bound for small n), run heuristic, assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: Delivery time windows are computed correctly from inter-stop distance and configured road-type average speed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets formula correctness (time = distance/speed plus window accumulation), typically within calculation logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies deterministic inputs (distance, configured speed) and expected computation, not integration with real infra.
behaviors.test_description: Call time-estimation logic with table-driven cases across road types, distances, and rounding boundaries; assert per-leg duration and cumulative window timestamps match expected values.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes exceeding remaining shift and returns exact excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is validator arithmetic/threshold logic. REAL-INFRA: not needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is a deterministic validation rule (compare total estimated time vs remaining shift, return excess).
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with edge cases (equal limit, just-over, far-over, fractional minutes) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess duration.

behaviors.description: If one address has no geocoding result, service skips it, optimizes remaining stops, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is at module handoff (geocoding result -> filtering -> optimizer input -> reporting), not just one function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; a stub geocoder returning "no results" reproduces failure. (b) no AI/ML output dependence. (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text says "API returns no results" (content handling), not transport failures/timeouts requiring real HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with in-process geocoder stub where one address returns empty result and others return coordinates; execute full route optimization call; assert skipped list contains failed address, failed address absent from optimized route, and remaining addresses are still routed.

behaviors.description: Duplicate input addresses are consolidated into one stop with merged delivery items and visited once.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is cross-module correctness (dedup/merge state must propagate into routing so stop is not revisited). Unit could verify merge logic but miss route-level double-visit regressions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required. (b) no AI/ML dependence. (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text concerns internal consolidation and route construction, not real infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with repeated address entries carrying distinct items; assert output has a single stop for that address, merged item set/count is correct, and route contains that stop exactly once.
